title: regreSSHion: RCE in OpenSSH server
description: A signal handler race condition in OpenSSH's sshd allows unauthenticated remote code execution as root on glibc-based Linux systems.

title: RCE in OpenSSH via ssh-agent PKCS#11
description: Remote code execution in OpenSSH's ssh-agent when forwarding to an attacker-controlled system, via PKCS#11 provider loading.

title: Privilege escalation in OpenSSH
description: Privilege escalation in OpenSSH via supplementary group handling when AuthorizedKeysCommand or AuthorizedPrincipalsCommand is used.

title: ProxyJump/ProxyCommand escape in OpenSSH
description: OpenSSH ProxyJump and ProxyCommand may allow a malicious server to inject commands via hostname arguments.

title: scp command injection in OpenSSH
description: OpenSSH scp client allows command injection via crafted filenames due to improper shell escaping in scp.c.

title: OpenSSH host key oracle
description: OpenSSH client may leak information about host key algorithms via the host key verification process, enabling passive fingerprinting.

title: scp path traversal in OpenSSH
description: OpenSSH scp client allows path traversal and overwrite of arbitrary files via crafted directory returned by the server.

title: OpenSSH null pointer dereference
description: OpenSSH sshd allows denial of service via null pointer dereference in the sshkey_check_revoked function.

title: OS command injection in OpenSSH
description: OpenSSH ProxyCommand and LocalCommand options allow OS command injection via crafted hostnames when using the ssh_config Match exec directive.

title: Information leak in OpenSSH
description: OpenSSH may leak sensitive information via timing side-channel in the public key authentication process.

title: Use-after-free in Linux netfilter
description: A use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel's netfilter subsystem allows local privilege escalation to root.

title: Local privilege escalation in Linux kernel
description: A vulnerability in the Linux kernel's nf_tables subsystem allows local privilege escalation via use-after-free.

title: Dirty Pipe: Linux kernel privilege escalation
description: A flaw in the Linux kernel's pipe subsystem allows unprivileged users to write to arbitrary files, leading to privilege escalation.

title: Linux kernel nf_tables use-after-free
description: A use-after-free in the Linux kernel's nf_tables allows local privilege escalation to root.

title: Linux kernel nf_tables out-of-bounds
description: An out-of-bounds access in the Linux kernel's netfilter nf_tables subsystem allows local privilege escalation.

title: eBPF verifier out-of-bounds in Linux kernel
description: An out-of-bounds read/write in the eBPF verifier of the Linux kernel allows local privilege escalation.

title: Ubuntu overlayfs privilege escalation
description: A privilege escalation vulnerability in Ubuntu's Linux kernel overlayfs implementation allows unprivileged users to gain root access.

title: XZ Utils backdoor (supply chain)
description: Malicious code inserted into XZ Utils (liblzma) via a complex supply chain attack, enabling unauthorized remote SSH access.

title: Use-after-free in Linux kernel
description: A use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel's netfilter subsystem can lead to local privilege escalation.

title: Use-after-free in Linux netfilter
description: A use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel's netfilter subsystem allows local privilege escalation.

title: SSRF via OpenSSL BIO_new_connect
description: A vulnerability in OpenSSL's SSL_select_next_proto and BIO_new_connect functions allows server-side request forgery.

title: Use-after-free in OpenSSL
description: A use-after-free vulnerability in OpenSSL's handling of certain TLS handshake messages can lead to denial of service or potential code execution.

title: Use-after-free in OpenSSL TLS
description: A use-after-free vulnerability in OpenSSL during TLS handshake processing of certain certificate messages.

title: Unbounded memory growth in OpenSSL
description: A vulnerability in OpenSSL causing unbounded memory growth when handling certain TLS session ticket messages.

title: X.509 policy check bypass in OpenSSL
description: A vulnerability in OpenSSL's X.509 certificate policy checks allows bypass of certificate verification.

title: DH key processing in OpenSSL
description: A vulnerability in OpenSSL's DH key processing that can lead to denial of service via excessive computation.

title: AES OCB mode encryption in OpenSSL
description: A vulnerability in OpenSSL's AES OCB mode implementation can lead to incorrect authentication tag computation.

title: Buffer overrun in OpenSSL X.509
description: A buffer overrun vulnerability in OpenSSL's X.509 certificate verification can lead to denial of service or potential code execution.

title: Buffer overrun in OpenSSL X.509
description: A buffer overrun vulnerability in OpenSSL's X.509 certificate verification that can be triggered with a crafted email address.

title: DoS in OpenSSL BN_mod_sqrt
description: A denial of service vulnerability in OpenSSL's BN_mod_sqrt function leads to infinite loop when parsing crafted certificates.

title: OpenSSL c_rehash command injection
description: A command injection vulnerability in OpenSSL's c_rehash script allows arbitrary command execution via crafted file names.

title: MIPS32 BN_nist_mod_256 side-channel
description: A side-channel vulnerability in OpenSSL's MIPS32 BN_nist_mod_256 function that could leak key material.

title: POLY1305 MAC bypass in OpenSSL
description: A vulnerability in OpenSSL's POLY1305 MAC implementation that could result in incorrect authentication tag computation.

title: runc container escape in Docker
description: A vulnerability in runc allows container escape via crafted process arguments, enabling access to the host system.

title: Moby auth bypass in Docker
description: A vulnerability in Docker's Moby authorization plugin framework allows privilege escalation via restricted actions.

title: Buildkit container escape in Docker
description: A vulnerability in Docker Buildkit allows container escape via crafted build arguments during image building.

title: Information leak in Docker
description: An information disclosure vulnerability in Docker allows leaking of sensitive data from the host to containers.

title: Command injection in Docker
description: A command injection vulnerability in Docker CE allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via crafted API requests.

title: Access control bypass in Docker
description: An access control bypass vulnerability in Docker allows containers to access host resources beyond intended limits.

title: Network vulnerability in Docker
description: A network isolation vulnerability in Docker allows containers to access host network services when using bridge networking.

title: runc container escape in Docker
description: A container escape vulnerability in runc allows malicious containers to overwrite the host runc binary and gain root access.

title: Buildkit container escape in Docker
description: A vulnerability in Docker Buildkit allows container escape via crafted build cache mounts.

title: Buildkit container escape in Docker
description: A high-severity vulnerability in Docker Buildkit allows container escape via crafted build steps.

title: Sudo qualification bypass
description: A vulnerability in Sudo's qualification handling allows bypass of authorization rules via crafted input.

title: Sudo privilege escalation
description: A privilege escalation vulnerability in Sudo allows a user to run arbitrary commands as root via specially crafted sudoedit invocation.

title: Baron Samedit: Sudo heap overflow
description: A heap-based buffer overflow in Sudo allows any unprivileged user to gain root access via a crafted sudo command.

title: Sudo auth bypass
description: An authentication bypass vulnerability in Sudo allows certain users to execute commands without proper authentication.

title: Sudo information leak
description: An information disclosure vulnerability in Sudo allows leakage of sensitive data via error messages.

title: Sudo auth bypass
description: An authentication bypass in Sudo allows unintended command execution by users with certain sudoers configurations.

title: Shellshock: Bash remote code execution
description: A remote code execution vulnerability in Bash via specially crafted environment variables passed to the shell.

title: Shellshock variant: Bash RCE
description: An additional Shellshock vulnerability in Bash allows remote code execution via crafted function definitions.

title: Shellshock variant: Bash RCE
description: Another Shellshock variant in Bash allowing remote code execution through crafted environment variables.

title: Bash privilege escalation
description: A vulnerability in Bash allows privilege escalation when Bash is invoked with certain SUID configurations.

title: Bash heap overflow
description: A heap-based buffer overflow in Bash allows denial of service or potential code execution via crafted input.

title: Bash out-of-bounds write
description: An out-of-bounds write in Bash allows denial of service or potential code execution via crafted heredoc input.

title: DNS vulnerability in systemd
description: A vulnerability in systemd-resolved allows denial of service via crafted DNS responses causing excessive resource consumption.

title: DNS vulnerability in systemd
description: A vulnerability in systemd-resolved allows denial of service via crafted DNS messages causing resource exhaustion.

description: An information disclosure vulnerability in systemd allows leakage of sensitive data via the journal logging system.

title: Information leak in systemd

description: A buffer overflow vulnerability in systemd allows denial of service or potential code execution via crafted service files.

title: ASN.1 parsing in curl
description: A vulnerability in curl's ASN.1 parsing for TLS certificates can lead to denial of service via crafted certificates.

title: Cookie vulnerability in curl

title: urllib vulnerability in Python

description: A vulnerability in CPython's venv module allows privilege escalation via improper handling of virtual environment creation.

description: A vulnerability in Python's zipfile module allows denial of service via crafted zip archives causing excessive resource consumption.

description: A server-side request forgery vulnerability in Apache HTTP Server allows access to internal network resources via crafted HTTP headers.
